title: Add recipe/prerequisite diagnostics to craft error handling
description:
## Problem
When a craft action fails in the Mineflayer adapter, the error is a bare "No recipe found for: torch" with no diagnostic information about WHY no recipe was found. This makes debugging opaque and forces manual investigation each time.

## Context
From the deep code audit (Finding 2): the adapter's event payload is too shallow for the C# side to reason about failures. When `bot.recipesFor()` returns empty, the adapter should emit structured context about what was checked.

## Requirements
1. When `bot.recipesFor()` returns empty, log structured diagnostics:
   - Item ID and name from registry
   - Whether `recipesFor` was called with/without crafting table filter
   - Current inventory of relevant ingredients (coal, stick, etc.)
   - Minecraft version and registry recipe count
2. Emit a structured `craftDiagnostic` event (not just throw) with this context so the C# side can log it
3. Optionally check prerequisites explicitly before calling `recipesFor()` and report which are missing

## References
- `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` lines 805-842
- Audit Finding 2 (observation depth) and Finding 6 (action progress telemetry)
- `Data/Pages/Tasks/handoff-torch-recipe-sprint36.md`
